items.headline: China brokers ceasefire talks between Pakistan and Afghanistan after weeks of deadly border clashes
items.body: Pakistan confirmed peace talks with Afghanistan's Taliban government in China, where Beijing is trying to broker a lasting ceasefire after weeks of fighting. The talks follow border violence that has disrupted trade and cross-border travel between nuclear-armed Pakistan and Afghanistan.

items.headline: Philippines declares world's first national energy emergency as oil dependency meets Middle East war
items.body: President Marcos declared a state of national energy emergency, making the Philippines the first country in the world to do so. The country imports 98 percent of its oil from the Middle East, leaving 115 million people critically vulnerable as closure of the Strait of Hormuz endangers supply.

items.headline: Burkina Faso government forces killed twice as many civilians as jihadist groups, rights investigators find
items.body: Burkina Faso military with allied militias killed more than 1,200 civilians since 2023, while jihadist groups killed around 600, according to new investigations. The pattern could bolster political legitimacy of armed groups and fuel recruitment, analysts warn.

items.headline: India launches world's largest census with caste enumeration for first time in nearly a century
items.body: India began counting its population in a year-long census costing 1.24 billion dollars, during which three million officials will survey 1.4 billion people. The count includes caste enumeration for the first time since 1931, which could trigger redrawn parliamentary maps and overhauled welfare quotas.
